title: Day 12 Quiz — Context, Reducer, and Immutability
description: Verify immutable updates, cart types, Context API, and useReducer patterns.

questions.prompt: You can safely mutate state.items with push() if you call setState afterward.
questions.explanation: Mutating then setState with same reference may skip re-render. Always return new array/object references.

questions.prompt: What problem does React Context solve?

options.text: Prop drilling — passing props through many intermediate components

questions.explanation: Context shares values deep in the tree without passing props at every level.

questions.prompt: What does useReducer provide over useState for cart logic?

options.text: Centralized predictable state transitions with typed actions

questions.explanation: Reducer consolidates complex update logic. useState works with Context too — reducer scales better for carts.

questions.prompt: Which are immutable cart updates? Select all that apply.
questions.explanation: filter, spread append, and map with spread item create new references. push mutates in place.

questions.prompt: Create Context with {{blank1}}. Read it in components with {{blank2}}.
questions.explanation: createContext defines the context; useContext reads the nearest provider value.

questions.prompt: After dispatch ADD_ITEM for id 1 twice (item already exists with quantity 1), what is quantity?
questions.explanation: ADD_ITEM should increment existing item quantity, not add duplicate row.

questions.prompt: Why throw an error in useCart() when context is null?

options.text: Fail fast when hook used outside CartProvider — clearer debugging

questions.explanation: Explicit error beats silent undefined behavior when provider is missing.

questions.prompt: When should you NOT use Context for state management?
questions.sampleAnswer: Avoid Context for frequently changing state consumed by many components — causes wide re-renders. Local state belongs in useState. Very large apps often move to Redux or split contexts. Do not put everything in one context.
questions.explanation: Context is for shared moderate-frequency state like cart, theme, auth.
Discriminated unions use literal type strings to narrow action payloads in reducer.

questions.prompt: Cart total should be calculated as:

options.text: Sum of price × quantity for each item

questions.explanation: Line total is price × quantity. Cart total sums all line totals.
